title: Write your own assert_equal
prompt_md:
Every test framework boils down to one idea: compare what you **got** to what you **want**.

Write `assert_equal(got, want)` that returns the string `"PASS"` when `got == want`, otherwise returns a failure message in the exact form:

```
FAIL: expected {want}, got {got}
```

Use normal Python `str()` formatting of the values (so `2` formats as `2`).
hints: Compare with `==`. If equal, return the literal string "PASS".
Build the failure string with an f-string: f"FAIL: expected {want}, got {got}".
